executiveSummary.text: Strait of Hormuz reopened — Iran declares passage 'completely open' during ceasefire; oil crashes 10%+ to ~$82 WTI, risk-off reversal in energy
S&P 500 hits 7,100 all-time high, Nasdaq longest win streak since 1992 — markets pricing in end of Iran conflict
TSMC & ASML report blowout Q1 but stocks sell off — sky-high expectations now the hurdle, not fundamentals. AI demand 'extremely robust'
Gold at $4,831/oz, copper at $6.10/lb near record — structural inflation + AI demand thesis intact, commodities remain scarcity play
Fed rate cuts delayed — Cleveland Fed inflation nowcasts climbing on energy shock; 'higher for longer' back on the table

usGlobal.what: TSMC Q1 profit up 58% YoY, HPC/AI hits 61% of revenue, gross margin 66%. Raised annual forecast. Stock fell ~3% on 'buy the rumor, sell the news'.
usGlobal.why: Core scarcity holding — AI demand is not slowing. Sell-off is expectations reset, not fundamentals deterioration. Capex $52-56B signals massive capacity build.
usGlobal.implication: Hold TSM firmly. The 'sell the news' pattern is familiar — same thing happened to NVDA last quarter. Capacity remains the gating factor, not demand.

usGlobal.what: ASML raised 2026 revenue forecast on AI demand, but stock dropped 6.5% — EUV machine targets (80 units in 2027 vs hoped 90) disappointed. China sales shrinking.
usGlobal.why: Key equipment monopoly in scarcity thesis. ASML is the only company making EUV machines at $400M each. Disappointment is on pace, not direction.
usGlobal.implication: ASML weakness is a buying opportunity. The EUV machine constraint IS the moat — demand far outstrips supply for the foreseeable future.

usGlobal.what: Lam Research (LRCX) reporting Q3 earnings Wed 4/22 — expected $5.7B revenue (+21% YoY), $1.35 EPS (+30% YoY). Bank of America names LRCX among top 8 semiconductor stocks.
usGlobal.why: Direct portfolio holding in equipment space. Strong earnings would validate the capex cycle thesis.
usGlobal.implication: Watch LRCX earnings closely — beat could lift the entire equipment complex (KLAC, AMAT). Miss would signal capex caution.

usGlobal.what: Micron (MU) stock takes a breather after strong April rally, down 4% on profit-taking. AI memory demand narrative remains intact.
usGlobal.why: Memory is the overlooked layer of AI infrastructure — HBM demand from NVDA's next-gen chips driving MU's growth.
usGlobal.implication: MU pullback is healthy consolidation. Hold position — HBM ramp is a 2026-2027 story.

usGlobal.what: Nvidia-OpenAI $100B deal is 'on ice' per WSJ (Jan report). Meanwhile, Nvidia launched Ising for quantum computing (Apr 14), Cerebras filed for IPO, and Nvidia denied PC maker acquisition rumor.
usGlobal.why: NVDA ecosystem expansion continues despite individual deal stumbles. Quantum play via Ising is a new vertical.
usGlobal.implication: NVDA remains the center of the AI universe. Individual deal delays don't change the infrastructure demand thesis. Ising/quantum is a long-term option.

singapore.what: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT preferential offering sees 170% excess applications — massive oversubscription for $900M fund raise. Units listing April 23.
singapore.why: Direct REIT holding. Oversubscription signals strong institutional demand for quality S-REITs despite rate headwinds.
singapore.implication: Bullish signal for CLAR and S-REITs broadly. The $1.4B acquisition pipeline + oversubscription shows institutional conviction. Hold and consider adding on weakness.

singapore.what: OCBC outpacing DBS and UOB in 2026 stock performance. OCBC 4.3% dividend yield still attractive. DBS guided 2026 net profit slightly below 2025 on rate headwinds.
singapore.why: Core Singapore bank holdings. OCBC's relative outperformance is notable but DBS still leads on dividend yield.
singapore.implication: Singapore banks remain foundation holdings. Rate headwind is real but wealth management and fee income offsetting. No action needed.

singapore.what: Keppel (BN4) analysts revising target price upward to ~S$14 on strong earnings prospects. Ex-dividend date April 27 (S$0.21). Stock at S$11.93.
singapore.why: Keppel is a transition story — from offshore to data centers, renewables and urban development. Earnings momentum building.
singapore.implication: Keppel upgrading thesis playing out. Hold through dividend. Target upside ~17% from current.

commodities.what: Gold at $4,831/oz — structural inflation regime + geopolitical risk + central bank buying keeping bid firm. Silver at $78.63/oz.
commodities.why: Core scarcity holding. Gold/Silver ratio at ~61 — silver still relatively cheap vs gold. Permanent inflation narrative is KL's thesis vindicated.
commodities.implication: Hold metals firmly. Inflation is 'permanent' per Kitco analysis. Oil crash doesn't break the structural inflation story — it's energy-independent now.

commodities.what: Copper surging near $6.10/lb (+9.9% MTD, +32.6% YoY). AI data center demand + supply shortage = structural bull. S&P Global predicts 50% demand increase by 2040.
commodities.why: Copper is the 'new oil' for AI infrastructure. Data centers alone adding 110K tons additional demand by 2026. Supply can't keep up.
commodities.implication: Copper position is the smartest trade in the portfolio. This is the AI infrastructure bottleneck nobody talks about. Hold and potentially add.

commodities.what: Uranium/nuclear stocks rallying — Iran war energy shock accelerating nuclear adoption in Asia and Africa. Uranium term prices at $90-93/lb.
commodities.why: Uranium thesis getting a geopolitical catalyst. Nuclear is the only scalable zero-carbon baseload for AI data centers.
commodities.implication: Uranium holding thesis strengthening. The Middle East conflict has made energy security a national priority — nuclear is the answer.

commodities.what: Oil crashes 10%+ on Strait of Hormuz reopening. WTI to ~$82, Brent to ~$88. But U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in effect.
commodities.why: Oil price directly impacts inflation trajectory and S-REIT yields. Quick de-escalation is bullish for equities, bearish for energy shorts.
commodities.implication: Oil crash removes near-term inflation acceleration risk BUT the structural inflation story (wage growth, deglobalization, AI capex) remains. Don't overreact to oil moves.

narratives.title: AI Capex Supercycle Intact Despite Profit-Taking
narratives.description: TSMC and ASML confirm AI spending boom with raised guidance, but 'sell the news' hits stocks. Big Tech (MSFT, META, AMZN, GOOG) expected to spend $600B+ on data centers in 2026. The bottleneck is supply, not demand — capacity constraints across TSMC, ASML, and advanced packaging. Earnings from LRCX, INTC, and GE Vernova this week will further test the thesis.

narratives.title: Geopolitical De-escalation: Strait of Hormuz Opens
narratives.description: Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz during ceasefire, oil crashes 10%+. Trump says 'most points already negotiated' for a lasting deal. Markets rally hard but risks remain — U.S. naval blockade still active, ceasefire fragile, no public deal details. If deal holds through month-end, it removes the biggest macro risk of 2026.

narratives.title: Structural Inflation Regardless of Oil
narratives.description: Cleveland Fed inflation nowcasts climbing even before oil shock. Fed minutes show 'some' officials favoring rate hikes. Kitco analysis: 'permanent inflation reshaping markets.' The key insight — even with oil normalizing, wage growth, deglobalization, AI capex-driven demand, and copper/uranium scarcity keep inflation elevated. Rate cuts delayed, possibly to 2027.

narratives.title: Copper: The Hidden AI Infrastructure Play
narratives.description: Copper at $6.10/lb, +32.6% YoY. AI data centers driving 110K tons additional demand. S&P Global forecasts 50% demand increase by 2040. Supply deficit structural — not enough new mines coming online. This is the scarcity thesis in its purest form: own what AI depends on.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil crash reduces near-term inflation pressure, but the Fed may not cut rates anyway — 'higher for longer' narrative gets second wind from structural inflation data. S-REITs face continued yield competition.
secondOrderEffects.implication: S-REIT dividend yield advantage narrows if rates stay high. Focus on REITs with growth (CLAR's acquisitions) rather than pure yield plays.

secondOrderEffects.text: Strait of Hormuz reopening weakens the oil/nuclear energy narrative short-term, but the nuclear thesis was always structural (AI data center power needs), not just geopolitical.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Don't sell uranium on oil price crash. The long-term demand driver is data center electricity, not Middle East geopolitics.

secondOrderEffects.text: TSMC's shift away from mature/lagging-edge to leading-edge is a margin expansion story — they're choosing which customers to serve, pricing power is immense.
secondOrderEffects.implication: TSMC is becoming the toll collector of the AI economy. This margin expansion story is underappreciated in current stock reaction.

secondOrderEffects.text: Advanced packaging (CoWoS) is the new bottleneck — TSMC ramping 2 new facilities in Taiwan + 2 in Arizona. Intel positioning as alternative packaging supplier (Amazon, SpaceX, Tesla as clients).
secondOrderEffects.implication: Equipment suppliers (LRCX, KLAC, AMAT) benefit from both front-end AND packaging capacity expansion. Intel's packaging entry is a potential competitive shift to watch.

investment.thesis: Add copper exposure on any pullback — copper is the bottleneck commodity for AI infrastructure that nobody is talking about enough
investment.whyNow: AI data center demand is structural and accelerating, supply deficit worsening, +32.6% YoY with no sign of slowing
investment.invalidator: Global recession destroying AI capex cycle

investment.thesis: Accumulate ASML on weakness — EUV monopoly with demand far outstripping supply for the foreseeable future
investment.trigger: ASML drops below recent support on 'disappointing' EUV unit guidance
investment.whyNow: Stock sold off 6.5% on numbers that were actually strong — classic expectations reset creates entry point
investment.invalidator: Lithography technology disruption or China export ban expansion

trading.thesis: Long LRCX into Q3 earnings (Wed 4/22) — expected beat with 21% revenue growth and 30% EPS growth
trading.whyNow: Bank of America top pick, strong semiconductor equipment cycle, TSMC/ASML guidance confirms capex boom
trading.invalidator: LRCX guides lower on customer pushback

trading.thesis: Short oil / trim energy exposure — Hormuz opening removes the geopolitical premium, WTI could test $75
trading.whyNow: Strait of Hormuz open, ceasefire in effect, Trump says deal 'very quickly' — geopolitical premium evaporating
trading.invalidator: Ceasefire collapses or Iran reverses Hormuz access

watchlist.thesis: Tesla (TSLA) earnings Wed 4/22 — 32% YoY earnings growth expected, AI/robotaxi narrative building
watchlist.trigger: Post-earnings reaction — watch for robotaxi timeline updates
watchlist.whyNow: After weeks of selling, TSLA bouncing into earnings. Not a portfolio holding but sentiment driver for risk assets

watchlist.thesis: Intel (INTC) earnings Thu 4/23 — advanced packaging opportunity, Cerebras IPO competition
watchlist.trigger: Packaging revenue disclosure and foundry customer updates
watchlist.whyNow: Intel's packaging division is the only credible alternative to TSMC CoWoS. If they show traction, it changes the competitive landscape for AMAT/LRCX equipment demand
watchlist.invalidator: Continued foundry losses and customer attrition

riskWatch.name: Ceasefire Fragility
riskWatch.description: Iran-Israel ceasefire is temporary and fragile. U.S. naval blockade still active. If ceasefire collapses, Strait of Hormuz could close again, oil spikes back above $100, markets crash. The 12-day oversold-to-overbought rally is built on hope, not a signed deal.
riskWatch.mitigation: Maintain gold and commodity hedge. Don't over-rotate from defensives to risk. Keep cash buffer at 26%.

riskWatch.name: Fed 'Higher for Longer' Trap
riskWatch.description: Structural inflation (even without oil shock) is keeping the Fed from cutting. Minutes show some officials want rate HIKES, not cuts. If inflation stays above 3%, rate cuts may not come until 2027. This pressures S-REITs, growth valuations, and SGD-denominated assets.
riskWatch.mitigation: Favor assets with pricing power (TSMC, semis) and inflation beneficiaries (copper, uranium, gold). Underweight pure rate-sensitive plays.

riskWatch.name: Semiconductor Expectations Reset
riskWatch.description: TSMC and ASML sold off on strong results. The bar is now so high that merely beating estimates isn't enough. If LRCX, TXN, or INTC disappoint, it could trigger a sector-wide rotation out of semis.
riskWatch.mitigation: Don't chase semiconductor rallies into earnings. Use sell-offs as accumulation opportunities. Focus on the capacity constraint thesis, not quarterly beats.

riskWatch.name: Rapid Overbought Condition
riskWatch.description: Piper Sandler warns market went from oversold to overbought in 12 days with 'lagging intermediate breadth.' This creates a 'highly fragile technical foundation' at all-time highs.
riskWatch.mitigation: Don't FOMO into new positions at ATH. Wait for pullbacks. The cash buffer exists for this reason.

keyEvents.text: Strait of Hormuz reopened by Iran during ceasefire — oil crashes 10%+, stocks rally to ATH
TSMC Q1: +58% YoY profit, HPC/AI 61% of revenue, margin 66% — stock sells off 3%
ASML raises 2026 revenue forecast, stock drops 6.5% on EUV unit disappointment
S&P 500 hits 7,100 ATH, Nasdaq longest win streak since 1992
CLAR preferential offering 170% oversubscribed — strong institutional demand for S-REITs
Fed inflation nowcasts climbing — rate cuts delayed, 'some' officials want hikes
Earnings week ahead: TSLA (Wed), INTC (Thu), LRCX (Wed), BA (Wed), UNH (Tue)
Gold $4,831/oz, Copper $6.10/lb near records — structural inflation trade intact

bottomLine.whatMatters: The AI capex supercycle is CONFIRMED by TSMC and ASML — demand outstrips supply, not the other way around. The Strait of Hormuz opening is a near-term relief rally catalyst but the structural stories (inflation, copper scarcity, nuclear energy) transcend geopolitics. Markets are at ATH on hope of peace, not a signed deal.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold all core positions firmly — the scarcity thesis is working. Use any semiconductor sell-offs as accumulation opportunities (especially ASML, TSM). Watch LRCX earnings Wednesday for equipment cycle validation. Consider adding copper exposure on pullbacks. Keep the 26% cash buffer — it's there for a reason and the market is overbought.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Short-term oil price volatility (the structural inflation story doesn't depend on oil). TSMC/ASML post-earnings sell-offs (expectations resets, not fundamental deteriorations). Daily market noise around Iran ceasefire headlines (wait for a signed deal, not tweets).
